exemplar.title: Painting a mural for a therapy center

detailedExemplarData.title: Painting a mural for a therapy center

strengths: Effective use of active listening with the art-therapy employee, showing collaboration and openness to expert guidance.
Reflective schedule adjustment after underestimating paint mixing times, revealing strong time-management adaptability.
Consistent application of time-management tools, illustrating ongoing self-monitoring and task prioritization.
weaknesses: Insufficient metrics to demonstrate the impact of ATL skills on outcome qualityâfor example, no data on improved accuracy after grid exercises.
Supervisor meetings are irregular and undocumented; a structured check-in schedule with recorded outcomes is needed.
Source evaluation insights are not explicitly linked to visual elements in sketches or color choices.
Integration of peer and expert feedback lacks before-and-after visual evidence, making it hard to see the direct improvements.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student shows clear engagement with ATL skillsâparticularly in time management and collaborationâadapting their plan and learning from feedback. To elevate this work, they should introduce quantitative evidence of skill impact, formalize expert consultations, and trace research or feedback directly to changes in the muralâs design through concrete before-and-after examples.

strengths: Well-justified material choices based on durability and color vibrancy, demonstrating thoughtful research into product performance.
Clear articulation of the muralâs intended dimensions and therapeutic purpose, providing a focused and meaningful project objective.
Comprehensive sequencing in the detailed plan, outlining step-by-step tasks that show strong organizational thinking.
weaknesses: Learning goal phrasing is vague; lacks measurable targets to track progress effectively.
ACCESS-FM table does not include quantitative feedback targets, making evaluation criteria unclear.
Contingency strategies and buffer periods are missing, reducing the planâs feasibility under unexpected delays.
Connection to personal interest is described broadly; specific emotional experiences and their influence on design choices are not detailed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning demonstrates a solid foundation through clear product definition and material reasoning, as well as a logically sequenced process. However, it falls short in establishing measurable learning goals and precise success criteria, and would benefit from explicit quantitative targets in the ACCESS-FM evaluation and the inclusion of contingency planning. Strengthening the personal interest link with concrete emotional anecdotes will deepen the rationale for design decisions.

strengths: Aesthetic evaluation supported by quantitative survey data, demonstrating an evidence-based approach to judging success.
Creative use of a visual reminder (mirror note) to maintain motivation, highlighting self-awareness and strategy application.
weaknesses: Reflection on academic learning lacks explicit linkage to IB learner-profile attributes and documented learning outcomes.
Discussion of environmental sustainability acknowledges shortcomings but omits actionable future steps such as sourcing eco-friendly materials.
Self-evaluation responses are lengthy and not consistently tied back to the defined success criteria, reducing evaluative clarity.
Skill-development reflection is general; specific examples or metrics (e.g., number of techniques mastered or hours practiced) are absent.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflective section is honest and shows initiative in using data and motivational strategies. However, it fails to connect insights to the IB framework, quantify learning growth, or offer concrete plans for environmental improvements. Tighter alignment with success criteria, explicit references to learner-profile attributes, and measurable evidence of skill development will deepen the evaluation.

annotations.comment: Insightful personal growth reflection; focus more tightly on academic learning outcomes and explicitly link them to IB learner-profile attributes for deeper criterion alignment.
Honest appraisal of environmental sustainability shortfalls; for future projects, outline actionable steps (e.g., sourcing eco-friendly paints) to improve your environmental performance.
Your self-evaluation questions frame meaningful reflection; make responses more concise and tie them directly to documented success criteria to enhance evaluative rigor.
Your aesthetic evaluation leverages quantitative survey data effectively; extend this data-driven approach to additional criteria (e.g., function, customer) for a comprehensive evaluation.
Well-justified material choices based on durability and color vibrancy; consider specifying exact paint brands or product codes to streamline procurement and budgeting.
You articulate the intended product dimensions and therapeutic purpose clearly, establishing a focused goal that will guide your research and painting process effectively.
The learning goal phrasing 'be familiar' is vague; specify measurable targets (e.g., complete three grid-scaling exercises by set dates) to make your success criteria more quantifiable and trackable.
Your ACCESS-FM table is comprehensive; to strengthen it, add quantitative targets for feedback (e.g., 80% positive responses) to ensure testing methods yield clear, measurable outcomes.
Good application of active listening with the art-therapy employee; to demonstrate impact, document metrics such as improved proportion accuracy after applying the grid method.
Your schedule adjustment after underestimating mixing times is well-articulated; consider specifying the number of hours reallocated or percentage improvement to quantify schedule resilience.
Identifying limited supervisor meetings shows reflective insight; plan regular, scheduled check-ins and record meeting outcomes to ensure consistent expert guidance in future.
Strong sequencing in your detailed plan; to improve feasibility, build in buffer periods for potential delays and outline contingency strategies to manage unforeseen challenges.
Introduction links to personal interest broadly; enhance this by describing specific emotional experiences and how they directly influenced your muralâs design choices.
Excellent use of time-management tools and reflective adjustments; consider logging task durations in your weekly schedule to provide clearer evidence of how you stayed on track.
Your source-evaluation strategy is robust; strengthen it further by explicitly linking key research insights to specific visual elements in your mural sketches or color palette.
Strong integration of peer and expert feedback; include before-and-after examples (e.g., draft vs final sketch) to concretely show how suggestions improved your composition.
Effective use of a visual reminder (mirror note) to sustain motivation; reflect on how this strategy quantitatively impacted your adherence to deadlines or task completion rates.
Innovative layering solution to rapid paint drying; to reinforce effectiveness, reference specific feedback or visual evidence showing how texture smoothness improved in later canvases.
Clear discussion of skill development; strengthen your reflection by citing specific examples or metrics (e.g., number of new techniques mastered) to evidence your learning progress.
